title: CRISPR-Cas9 Gene Editing in Yeast: A Molecular Biology and Bioinformatics Laboratory Module for Undergraduate and High School Students
application: HDR-mediated ADE2 ORF deletion using donor DNA
vectors: single-plasmid system containing constitutively active Cas9 and gRNA
assembly_method: Pre-cloned gRNA plasmids are used in the module; students design/analyze gRNAs, but the main paper does not describe a full plasmid cloning assembly workflow.
delivery_method: cotransformation of Cas9/gRNA plasmid and linear donor DNA
validation_methods: PCR amplification of edited ADE2 locus

design_rules.name: ADE2_target_selection
design_rules.description: Students identify SpCas9 PAMs and associated gRNA sequences in the ADE2 gene, first manually and then using Benchling CRISPR analysis.

design_rules.name: Cas9_PAM_rule
design_rules.description: Use Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9 targeting logic to identify PAM sequences, matching gRNA sequences, and corresponding Cas9 cut sites.

design_rules.name: Guide_score_comparison
design_rules.description: Compare candidate gRNA target locations and predicted on-target/off-target scores before choosing a guide.

design_rules.name: Multiple_guide_efficiency_rule
design_rules.description: Test different gRNAs targeting the same ADE2 gene because predicted scores may not match empirical editing efficiency.

design_rules.name: Donor_template_rule
design_rules.description: For precise ADE2 ORF deletion, cotransform a linear donor template with the Cas9+gRNA plasmid.

interpretation_notes: The main paper does not provide full construction-ready oligo sequences.
The paper references supplemental appendices for detailed student protocols, plasmids, and materials.
Best suited for shorthand construction workflow generation rather than direct sequence-level reconstruction.

key_constraints: Full plasmid sequences are not provided in the main paper.
The four ADE2 gRNA plasmids are described as previously cloned and ready to use.
Exact gRNA sequences and cloning oligos are not fully listed in the main text.
Detailed materials and methods are located in supplemental appendices.
Best suited for paper_shorthand mode rather than sequence_build mode.
notes: Students perform a dry lab using Saccharomyces Genome Database and Benchling to design and evaluate ADE2 gRNAs.
The experimental setup includes three transformations: Cas9-only control, Cas9+gRNA without donor, and Cas9+gRNA with donor.
Loss of ADE2 function produces a red colony phenotype, allowing visual scoring of editing outcomes.
Follow-up analysis can distinguish NHEJ and HDR outcomes by PCR and sequencing.
